title: Chilaquiles Red Salsa

seo.title: Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe: Authentic Mexican Breakfast
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Chilaquiles Red Salsa with our easy step-by-step recipe. Perfect for a hearty Mexican breakfast at home!
longTailKeywords: how to make Chilaquiles Red Salsa
Chilaquiles Red Salsa step by step
best Chilaquiles Red Salsa at home
seo.imageAlt: A plate of authentic Chilaquiles Red Salsa topped with crema, cheese, and onions, ready for a Mexican breakfast.
seo.imageCaption: Start your day with a vibrant and flavorful plate of homemade Chilaquiles Red Salsa!

description: Chilaquiles Rojos are a quintessential Mexican breakfast, offering a symphony of flavors and textures. Crispy corn tortilla pieces are bathed in a rich, spicy red salsa, creating a comforting and satisfying dish. Topped with fresh cheese, crema, and onion, it's a vibrant and hearty start to any day.

ingredients.notes: roughly chopped for salsa, plus more for garnish
for frying, plus more if needed

instructions.title: Prepare Salsa Ingredients
instructions.text: Place the Roma tomatoes, serrano peppers, and garlic cloves in a small saucepan. Cover with water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 10-12 minutes, or until the tomatoes are soft and their skins begin to split. Drain the water, reserving a little if needed for blending.

instructions.title: Blend the Red Salsa
instructions.text: Transfer the boiled tomatoes, serrano peppers, and garlic to a blender. Add the roughly chopped white onion, chicken broth, and salt. Blend until completely smooth. If the salsa is too thick, add a splash of the reserved cooking water or more broth until it reaches a pourable consistency.

instructions.title: Simmer the Salsa
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Pour the blended salsa into the hot oil. Bring the salsa to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the flavors to meld and the salsa to slightly thicken. Taste and adjust salt if necessary.

instructions.title: Fry the Tortilla Chips
instructions.text: While the salsa simmers, heat 1/2 cup of vegetable oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Once hot, carefully add a single layer of tortilla quarters. Fry for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and crispy. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil. Repeat with remaining tortillas.

instructions.title: Combine and Coat Chilaquiles
instructions.text: Carefully add the crispy tortilla chips to the simmering red salsa in the saucepan. Gently toss the tortillas with the salsa using tongs or a spatula, ensuring all the chips are well coated. Cook for 1-2 minutes, just long enough for the chips to soften slightly but still retain some bite. Do not overcook, or they will become soggy.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the Chilaquiles Red Salsa to individual plates. Garnish generously with crumbled queso fresco, a drizzle of Crema Mexicana, thinly sliced red onion, and fresh chopped cilantro. For an extra hearty meal, serve with a fried egg or shredded chicken on top.

tips: For extra flavor in your salsa, char the tomatoes, peppers, and onion on a comal or cast-iron skillet before blending, instead of boiling.
If you prefer a milder salsa, remove the seeds and veins from the serrano peppers before boiling. For more heat, leave them in or add an extra pepper.
Serve immediately to enjoy the perfect balance of crispy and saucy tortillas. If making ahead, keep salsa and chips separate and combine just before serving.
article:
Unleash the vibrant flavors of Mexico right in your kitchen with a dish that’s as comforting as it is exhilarating: Chilaquiles Red Salsa. Forget your mundane breakfast routine; it’s time to dive into a bowl of crispy tortilla chips drenched in a rich, spicy red sauce, adorned with an array of fresh toppings. This isn't just a meal; it's an experience, a celebration of Mexican culinary heritage perfect for starting any day with a flourish. If you’ve ever yearned for an authentic Mexican breakfast that truly satisfies, then learning this Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe is your next culinary adventure.

## What are Chilaquiles Red Salsa?

Chilaquiles (pronounced chee-lah-KEE-lehs) are a beloved traditional Mexican breakfast dish, though they are often enjoyed at any time of day, especially after a festive night. At its heart, chilaquiles consist of lightly fried or baked corn tortilla pieces, cut into quarters or triangles, simmered in a flavorful salsa until they soften slightly but still retain a pleasant bite. While green salsa (salsa verde) chilaquiles are popular, the red salsa version offers a deeper, often spicier, and more robust flavor profile, typically made from dried chiles like ancho and guajillo, combined with ripe tomatoes.

The beauty of Chilaquiles Red Salsa lies in its layers of texture and taste. The warm, saucy tortillas provide a comforting base, while the toppings elevate the dish to new heights. Common accompaniments include crumbled cotija cheese, a dollop of cooling crema (Mexican sour cream), thinly sliced white onion, fresh cilantro, and often a fried egg on top. The red salsa, with its smoky, earthy, and often fiery notes, is what truly defines this classic dish, making it a staple in homes and eateries across Mexico.

## Ingredients for Red Chilaquiles

Crafting the perfect Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe requires a selection of fresh, high-quality ingredients. Here's what you'll need to gather:

**For the Red Salsa:**
*   6-8 dried Guajillo chiles, stemmed and deseeded
*   2-3 dried Ancho chiles, stemmed and deseeded (for depth and mild heat)
*   2-3 ripe Roma tomatoes, halved
*   1/2 white onion, roughly chopped
*   2 cloves garlic, peeled
*   4 cups chicken or vegetable broth
*   1 tablespoon vegetable oil (for sautéing aromatics)
*   Salt to taste
*   1/2 teaspoon cumin powder (optional, for earthy notes)
*   1/4 teaspoon dried oregano (Mexican oregano preferred)

**For the Tortillas:**
*   12-15 corn tortillas (day-old work best), cut into 6-8 wedges each
*   Vegetable oil for frying (about 2-3 cups)

**For Topping & Serving:**
*   1/2 cup crumbled Cotija cheese (or queso fresco)
*   1/4 cup Mexican crema or sour cream
*   1/4 cup finely chopped white onion
*   1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped
*   2-4 fried eggs (optional, but highly recommended)
*   Sliced avocado (optional)
*   Refried beans or rice (for serving on the side)

## Step-by-Step Guide to Making Chilaquiles

Follow this detailed guide to create an unforgettable Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe from scratch:

**1. Prepare the Dried Chiles:**
*   Place the guajillo and ancho chiles in a heatproof bowl. Pour enough boiling water over them to submerge completely. Let them soak for 20-30 minutes until soft and pliable.
*   Once softened, drain the chiles, reserving about 1/2 cup of the soaking liquid.

**2. Roast Tomatoes, Onion, and Garlic:**
*   In a dry pan or on a comal over medium-high heat, roast the halved Roma tomatoes, chopped white onion, and peeled garlic cloves. Turn them occasionally until lightly charred and softened (about 5-7 minutes). This step adds a crucial smoky depth to your salsa.

**3. Blend the Red Salsa:**
*   Transfer the softened chiles, roasted tomatoes, onion, and garlic to a blender. Add 2 cups of the chicken or vegetable broth, the reserved chile soaking liquid (if needed for consistency), cumin powder, and oregano.
*   Blend until completely smooth. If the salsa is too thick, add more broth gradually until it reaches a pourable, slightly thick consistency.
*   Pass the blended salsa through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, pressing with a spoon to extract all the liquid and discard any remaining solids. This step ensures a silky-smooth salsa.

**4. Simmer the Salsa:**
*   Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Pour in the strained salsa.
*   Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low and cook for at least 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally. This allows the flavors to meld and deepen. Season generously with salt to taste.

**5. Fry the Tortilla Chips:**
*   While the salsa simmers, heat 2-3 cups of vegetable oil in a large, heavy-bottomed skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat until it reaches 350-375°F (175-190°C).
*   Working in batches, fry the tortilla wedges until golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per batch.
*   Using a slotted spoon, transfer the fried chips to a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil. Season lightly with salt.

**6. Assemble the Chilaquiles:**
*   In a large skillet or Dutch oven, pour about 1 cup of the hot red salsa. Add a batch of crispy tortilla chips and gently toss to coat, ensuring all chips are covered but not overly saturated. The goal is for them to soften slightly but still have some texture.
*   Cook for 1-2 minutes, just until the chips begin to absorb the salsa and soften. Do not overcook, or they will become mushy. Repeat with remaining chips and salsa if serving multiple plates.

**7. Plate and Garnish:**
*   Immediately transfer the saucy chilaquiles to serving plates.
*   Top generously with crumbled Cotija cheese, a drizzle of Mexican crema, chopped white onion, and fresh cilantro.
*   For an ultimate Mexican breakfast experience, add a perfectly fried egg on top of each serving.

## Tips for Perfect Red Chilaquiles

*   **Day-Old Tortillas:** For the best crispy chips that won't absorb too much oil, use slightly stale or day-old corn tortillas. Fresh tortillas tend to be too moist.
*   **Don't Overcook the Chips:** Fry them until they're golden and crisp, but not overly browned. Remember they will soften further in the salsa.
*   **Salsa Consistency is Key:** The salsa should be thick enough to coat the chips but thin enough to be absorbed. Adjust with broth if needed.
*   **Toasting vs. Soaking Chiles:** Some recipes call for toasting dried chiles before soaking. This can enhance their smokiness. If you choose to do this, toast them briefly in a dry pan until fragrant, being careful not to burn them.
*   **Serve Immediately:** Chilaquiles are best enjoyed hot and fresh. The longer they sit, the soggier the chips will become.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the number of ancho chiles (for mildness/earthiness) and guajillo chiles (for moderate heat) to your preference. For extra kick, add a small arbol chile or a pinch of cayenne pepper to the salsa.

## Serving Suggestions for Chilaquiles

While Chilaquiles Red Salsa is a meal in itself, there are several traditional and delicious ways to serve them:

*   **The Classic Way:** With a fried egg (or two!) on top, allowing the runny yolk to mix with the rich salsa.
*   **With Sides:** Serve alongside a generous portion of refried beans and Mexican rice for a truly hearty breakfast or brunch.
*   **Avocado:** Sliced or mashed avocado adds a creamy, fresh counterpoint to the spicy salsa.
*   **Grilled Meats:** For a more substantial meal, serve with grilled steak (carne asada) or shredded chicken.
*   **Coffee & Juice:** A strong cup of Mexican coffee and a refreshing glass of orange juice complete the authentic experience.

## Variations & Add-ins for Your Chilaquiles

The beauty of this Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe is its adaptability. Feel free to experiment and make it your own:

*   **Protein Power-Up:** Shredded cooked chicken, pork carnitas, or even beef barbacoa can be stirred into the chilaquiles with the salsa or piled on top.
*   **Cheese Please:** While Cotija is traditional, shredded Monterey Jack or Oaxaca cheese can be melted over the chilaquiles just before serving for a gooey, cheesy twist.
*   **Vegetarian Options:** Omit meat and ensure your broth is vegetable-based. Add black beans, corn, or sautéed bell peppers for extra texture and nutrition.
*   **Spicier Kick:** For those who love heat, incorporate a few chiles de árbol into your salsa blend, or serve with a side of your favorite hot sauce.
*   **Baked Tortillas:** For a healthier option, instead of frying, bake your tortilla wedges in a single layer at 375°F (190°C) for 10-15 minutes, flipping once, until crispy. This alters the texture slightly but still makes for delicious chilaquiles.
*   **Salsa Variations:** While we focus on the red salsa here, you can easily adapt the base recipe for green chilaquiles using tomatillos and serrano or jalapeño peppers.

Mastering this Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe will undoubtedly earn you rave reviews and transport your taste buds straight to the heart of Mexico. It's a dish that embodies comfort, flavor, and tradition, making it a perfect addition to your culinary repertoire. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to savor a truly spectacular Mexican breakfast.
